# Autogenerated Stations - General Information

## Description

With the Multicarrier Configuration editor, you can automatically generate stations on a Lexium™ MC multi carrier track.

For more information on the Multicarrier Configuration editor, refer to the [Lexium™ MC multi carrier Configuration Guide](../../../../../api/crossBook?lang=en-US&virtualBookName=MLSConfG&topicID=).

**Creating Station Types:**

The Station Types tab allows you to define three station types that you can assign to the stations defined in the Stations tab: clamping, declamping, and grouping stations.

**Defining Stations:**

The Stations tab allows you to create stations by selecting a station type and defining the properties of the stations. You can use the User Station default station type or you can select a station type as defined in the Station Types tab.

**Visualization and Generating Code:**

The Track tab allows you to verify the position of the stations on a Lexium™ MC multi carrier track.

When the parameters are set, you can generate the code by selecting the Update > To Code command in the Track tab.

NOTE: For using the Update > To Code operation for stations, you must activate the option Create Code for Stations in the Project > Project Settings > Multicarrier Configuration Editor > Code Generation tab.  

For details, refer to [Menu Commands Online Help](../../../../../api/crossBook?lang=en-US&virtualBookName=SoMMenu&topicID=TPC_MLS_Config_Tools_Options_D995A499)

The code generated is stored under the folder node MulticarrierStations that contains the subfolder Autogenerated Stations that, in turn, contains the following elements:

* A subfolder User Stations that contains a subfolder for each user station that is available in the Stations tab. For more information on user stations, refer to [FB\_UserStation](FB_UserStation-E44F4714.html#FB_UserStation-E44F4714).
* A subfolder Visualizations that contains one visualization frame for each station. For more information on available visualization frames, refer to [Visualization Frames](D-SE-0077977.html).
* The main program of the generated stations (`SR_CallStationsGenerated (PRG)`). The program contains the code to run the stations. For more information on the program, refer to [SR\_CallStationsGenerated](SRCallStationsGenerated-E3E2C410.html#SRCallStationsGenerated-E3E2C410).
